title: Button bug when workflow created
excerpt: There’s been a bug where this pops up even when the button is used in a workflow automation. This confuses people because it makes it seem like the workflow didn’t work. Can we get this fixed, please?

posts.author: cphu (Christine Phu)
posts.cookedHtml:
There’s been a bug where this pops up even when the button is used in a workflow automation. This confuses people because it makes it seem like the workflow didn’t work. Can we get this fixed, please?

image340×515 27.4 KB

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @cphu
I agree that would be confusing, especially if the workflow runs but the message makes it look like it didn’t.

I recommend reporting it to Support so they can review your specific setup and escalate if needed. Including a short screen recording will help them troubleshoot faster.

In the meantime, it may help to double check the button column and custom automation settings to confirm everything is configured as expected.

If you’d like hands-on help or want us to walk through this live, you can book a 1:1 paid 60-minute strategy session with our team here:
Calendly

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a great one to send to support. As a workaround I generally add a ‘normal automation’ when using buttons in workflows to keep that from popping up. ie When button is clicked, notify ‘me’ or ‘set date’ column. Something small that won’t hurt anything else, but at least gets that message to go away.

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Jmergen (Jessica Mergen)
posts.cookedHtml: I have noticed this problem as well. And like someone else noted in the comments, I did try setting (and then disabling) a simple automation for the button to try and trick it to go away. However, that doesn’t seem 100% reliable.
